Tash Sultana ignites Montreal jazz festival's opening outdoor blowout   The 42nd edition of the jazz fest has barely begun and the bar is already set very, very high.
Montreal opposition slams city for stalled social housing projects
What's open and closed on Canada Day, July 1, 2022
Popular Montreal restaurateur charged with uttering threats
Montreal jazz fest daily picks: July 1   Meshell Ndegeocello has always been impossible to pigeon-hole, which is why so many of us love her.
Weekend traffic: Section of Highway 40, Pie-IX bridge will close   Here's what to look out for over the Canada Day weekend.
Southbound La Fontaine tunnel closed after car catches fire   Crews are determining whether there was any serious damage to the structure of the tunnel.
Second man charged in connection with the killing of Meriem Boundaoui   Aymane Bouadi, 27, was charged with first-degree murder as well as four counts of attempted murder.

Air Canada to slash summer flight schedule as airports face lengthy delays   Three Air Canada routes will be temporarily suspended between Montreal and Pittsburgh, Baltimore and Kelowna, B.C., and one from Toronto to Fort McMurray, Alta., the carrier said.
Popular Montreal restaurateur charged with uttering threats
What's open and closed on Canada Day, July 1, 2022
Jordan Peterson suspended from Twitter, says it might as well be a ban: 'I won’t apologize'
Hitman tied to organized crime removed by helicopter from a federal penitentiary
Gallery: Aislin and other editorial cartoonists — July 2022

Kramberger: Fallout from drag-queen activity shows work on acceptance remains   There were enough hateful or threatening posts that Dorval officials added security for a second event held at the municipal library.
Martine St-Victor: Karim Ouellet deserves to rest in peace   Some of the details in the coroner's report released were shocking and macabre; all were intimate. And none of our business.
Opinion: We must shift the abortion conversation — it's about equality rights, pure and simple   Friday's U.S. Supreme Court decision casts an ominous shadow that reaches into Canada.
Opinion: Canada's flag has been through a lot, so fly it proudly on July 1   Canada is hardly perfect, but it’s the envy of nations around the world.

Montrealer Lu Dort to re-sign with OKC Thunder on five-year, $87.5M deal: Report   The club declined to pick up Dort’s $1.93 million option earlier this week, which helped set the stage for a richer extension.

Alouettes go-to receiver Eugene Lewis has sights set on CFL's top prize   While the league's outstanding player award almost always goes to a quarterback, Lewis is attempting to become only the third receiver since 2010 to capture the honour.

Toronto Raptors re-sign Montrealer Chris Boucher to three-year contract   Boucher averaged 9.4 points and 6.2 rebounds per game last season, and 11.2 points and 5.8 rebounds in six playoff games against the Philadelphia 76ers.
CF Montreal edges Sounders to move into tie atop conference   The victory moves Montreal (9-6-2, 29 points) into a tie with Philadelphia atop the Eastern Conference.

Stu Cowan: Remembering a time when there was a cloud of smoke in NHL   When Chris Nilan was with the Canadiens in the 1980s, his pregame routine included drinking coffee and smoking cigarettes with Guy Lafleur.
Lozeau photo store, a mainstay of Plaza St-Hubert, is shuttered   "Not only is the plaza losing an important member of its community, it's also a sad day for Quebec retail."
Airlines must cut flights to ease travel issues: Trudeau airport boss   Amid delays, cancelations, lineups and lost luggage, “it’s clear that we need to restore balance in the logistical chain."
Quebec poker champion comes up a winner in high-stakes showdown with CRA   Jonathan Duhamel's winnings from his gambling activities ultimately caught the eye of the tax man.

Quebec's Felix & Paul Studios to record launch of Artemis I mission to moon   The virtual reality production will be "completely immersive" and will allow spectators "to live the experience as if they were there."
Utica Resources files lawsuit seeking billions of dollars if Quebec implements Bill 21   The law makes Quebec the first North American jurisdiction to ban hydrocarbon exploration and production

Brownstein: Comedy community mourns passing of Montrealer Nick Nemeroff   “We’re still waiting for word as to what happened. There were no outward signs of anything. No one expected this. He was just way too young.”
Montreal's Clay and Friends pack party vibes to free jazz fest show   In a programming coincidence — or a passing of the torch — the band will play 24 hours before local icons Bran Van 3000.
Cinéma sous les étoiles launches in the Plateau Wednesday evening   Cinéma sous les étoiles, which began in 2010, will screen nearly 60 films in 20 parks and outdoor venues. It runs till Sept. 9.
CRTC orders Radio Canada to apologize for using N-word in 2020 program   A commentator made reference to a controversial book by Pierre Vallières that has the offensive word in its title.
Just for Laughs to honour Amy Schumer, Hasan Minhaj, among others   Schumer will be the recipient of the Comedy Person of the Year, while Minhaj has been named Stand-up Comedian of the Year.

Six O’Clock Solution: One-pot chicken cacciatore is quick and simple   Some recipes in Clodagh McKenna's new cookbook take only 10 minutes, others 20, and still others, like this one, 30 minutes to prepare.
Juhl: 3 road-trip games to keep the kids from driving you around the bend   These ideas will help you pass the time in the car and you don't have to buy anything to play.
Bill Zacharkiw's Wines of the Week: June 24, 2022   Every week, Bill Zacharkiw identifies his top wine picks available at the SAQ and offers ideas for food pairings.
The Right Chemistry: Caul fat — believe it's not butter   Why a Wisconsin senator didn't like the taste of beef fat, and what that has to do with regulating food colour.
Nawaz: Bugs are part of the family now, but those spiderwebs have to go   Creating educational opportunities out of housekeeping neglect does not make one’s dwelling conducive for guests.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.

True Crime Byline: Families knew 'horrible creature' was taking Vancouver women long before police did   The DNA of 33 missing women was found on Robert Pickton's pig farm. But it's the story of the victims that stays with me, not the killer.

True Crime Byline podcast: This Isn't a Story about Robert Pickton   Listen to this podcast episode for the heartbreaking history of Canada’s most prolific serial killer case, as told by Vancouver Sun reporter Lori Culbert.

Secrecy in the courts: Exclusive study reveals increasing use of publication bans in Canada   An investigation of discretionary publication bans requested during the past two years across four provinces shows a 25 per cent increase from one year to the next
Hyundai drops the Veloster N hot hatch for 2023   The regular Velosters were discontinued earlier, and now the N is leaving, too
Spied! Chevrolet Silverado EV spotted prowling the streets   Suddenly, a high-volume trim level appears
Apple eyes fuel purchases from dashboard as it revs up car software   A new feature will allow CarPlay users to tap an app to navigate to a pump and buy gas straight from a screen in the car
German court doubles red-light runner's fine due to SUV size   The penalty was set higher because the sport-ute allegedly posed more risk to pedestrians than a smaller car
Cross-Canada Road Trip: Whitehorse to Toronto in two 2022 Toyota Tundras   Some 5,500 km and seven days of driving through one magnificent country show the new-gen truck is just as stout as the old

Justin Bieber 'did something very important for the facial difference community'   Advocate Alim Somji says the pop star challenges the typical images of facial difference portrayed in the media as villains, such as Scar from the movie The Lion King.

WATCH: We take our medications out of the bathroom thanks to this TikTok trend   A bathrooms isn't a great storage option for meds, as the swings in temperatures and moist conditions can cause them to lose potency.
What are the signs of pancreatitis?   Acute and chronic pancreatitis are typically diagnosed between the ages of 40 and 70, with men more likely to be diagnosed across all age groups.

Machado: COVID has made socializing a little like having sex without protection   Getting back to some kind of normal means accepting the risk of COVID, and understanding that the feeling of liberation comes along with fear and uncertainty.
What are the signs of Parkinson's disease?   For people living with Parkinson's disease, exercise and eating a balanced diet may help control symptoms and even slow progression of the disease.
Ontario man accused of impaired driving after cannabis and alcohol found inside his vehicle   Man taken into custody after the OPP received multiple complaints about his driving on Highway 12
Over half of people using medical cannabis to combat chronic pain had withdrawal symptoms: study   Clusters of symptoms occur after significant periods of not using marijuana.
Florida man's idea to hide two cannabis cigars in his shoe was a bust   Man arrested for weed possession and driving with a suspended licence.

Toronto-based company partners with Wiz Khalifa on line of psychedelic and functional mushrooms   While the psychedelic mushrooms will only be available in the Netherlands, functional home-grow kits are set to launch in the U.S., Europe and Canada.

Lawyer looking to secure cannabis retail spot for client tried to bribe police chief   The lawyer approached a close relative of the police chief and offered that person $32,250 to speak to the chief.
